title: populus × canadensis var. aurea-vangeertii
intro: Populus × canadensis var. aurea-vangeertii, also known as the Golden Canadian Poplar, is a deciduous tree that is a cultivar of the Canadian poplar tree, typically grown for its ornamental value and unique golden-yellow foliage. The bark and leaves of the Golden Canadian Poplar have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as possessing antimicrobial and antifungal properties. Herbal preparations of the Golden Canadian Poplar can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it is also used in traditional herbal medicine to treat various ailments, such as digestive issues and skin conditions.
